2025 Audited Results & Upcoming Board Changes
Powerhouse Energy Group plc (AIM: PHE), a company pioneering integrated technology that converts non-recyclable waste into low carbon energy,
is pleased to announce its audited results for the year ended 31 December 2025.
Powerhouse also announces that Paul Emmitt, Chief Executive Officer, has confirmed that due to a change in family circumstances he will not be standing for re-election at the Company's Annual General Meeting expected to be held on Thursday 3rd September 2026 at 14.00pm. Accordingly, Mr Emmitt will step down from the Board and as CEO at the conclusion of the AGM, at which point his employment will terminate in accordance with his service agreement. It is intended Paul will continue to work with Engsolve on a technical consultancy basis, with the details to be confirmed. David Hitchcock will assume an Executive Chair role at conclusion of the AGM until a replacement CEO is identified.
Summary of 2025 Financial Results and post period events:
Corporate:
·
Strategy focused on the goals of delivering first projects and creating a qualified pipeline of opportunities - excellent progress has been made during the period
·
During the period the Company prioritised and, more importantly, validated projects to be progressed, both where Powerhouse would act as developer and those where it would licence its DMG technology
Commercial development:
·
Completion of Powerhouse Technology Centre in Bridgend, with the 2.5 tonne per day kiln for the Feedstock Testing Unit (FTU)
·
Following completion of the FTU, significant interest has been seen from a number of global stakeholders and companies, leading to a significant pipeline of opportunities on which the Company continues to follow up
·
Considerable progress made with National Hydrogen, Australia ("NH2"), with FEED being completed in June 2025. NH2 has now established the project SPV "National Waste to Energy", in which Powerhouse will hold a minimum of 5% equity stake
·
On Ballymena, a lease agreement has been signed with planning permission now submitted. Discussions continue with several potential investors / funders for Ballymena
·
Commenced an exciting new project with a Welsh battery developer, providing thermal processing expertise to modify test kilns and support the client in developing novel anode material
Technology and Innovation:
·
A number of key patents granted in a number of regions expected to become key markets for the Company
·
Six patents granted to date with no additional patent applications planned
Financial:
·
PHE's waste to energy division generated its first revenues in January 2026 when the Company secured a contract with a Welsh based battery developer
·
Revenue for 2025 was £1.23 million (2024: £499k), all derived from Engsolve activities.
·
The Group reported an operating loss of £2,932,369 (2024: £4,705,025).
·
Cash at bank at year end of £703,691 (2024: £1.31 million).
·
In 2025, Engsolve reported an increase in annual revenues from £1.38m in 2024 to £1.46m in 2025 (includes intercompany revenue).
·
Fundraises of £1.3m completed post the year end
Outlook:
·
Ballymena continues to advance well across multiple workstreams
·
Entered into a MENA, GCC and Asia Marketing Agreement with Green Gecko Energy Limited
·
Granted a non-exclusive license to Hydrogen Utopia International PLC to promote the Company's Distributed Modular Generation ("DMG") technology across selected European markets.
·
PHE participating in new Horizon Europe Innovation Action under HORIZON-CL6-2025-01-CIRCBIO-01: "JUST-CIRCLE - Measuring and Advancing Circular Business Models for a Fair and Sustainable Transition", which is being led by Brunel Business School at Brunel University of London.
·
Continued focus on marketing the FTU in 2026
·
Powerhouse currently has approximately several live enquiries, defined as opportunities the Company believes have potential to become revenue-generating projects
David Hitchcock, Non-Executive Chairman, commented:
"This has been a hugely significant year for the Company. Whilst being finished in March last year the completion of the FTU has enabled the Company to showcase its technology to the industry, potential clients and investors; and has led to a very significant increase in in-bound enquiries about our technology and its potential. We are delighted with the interest that we have received and even more so as a number of these enquiries have the potential to become very real revenue generating projects.
"The team have continued to build on this and since the year end, we have seen a number of initiatives that we believe will help drive the growth of the business going forward. This with the progress that we are seeing with our current projects at Ballymena and with NH2 lead us to believe that 2026 is promising to be another strong year for the Company.
"I would also like to take this opportunity to thank all our shareholders for their continued support during the year and look forward to welcoming you all again to our head office to see if FTU, if you have not already done so, as well as providing further updates in due course."
"I would particularly like to thank Paul for his service and determination that has enabled the Company to progress to this stage and look forward to continuing our relationship in his new capacity."
A full copy of the Company's Report and Accounts for the year ended 31 December 2025 will be sent to shareholders shortly and available on the Company's website
www.phegroup.com
.
The Notice of Annual General Meeting expected to be held on Thursday 3rd September 2026 at 14.00pm at a venue in London to be confirmed will be sent to shareholders shortly and will also be available on the Company's website.

About Powerhouse Energy Group Plc
Powerhouse Energy has developed a process technology which can utilise waste plastic, end-of-life-tyres, and other waste streams to convert them efficiently and economically into syngas from which valuable products such as chemical precursors, hydrogen, electricity, heat and other industrial products may be derived. PHE's process produces low levels of safe residues and requires a small operating footprint, making it suitable for deployment at enterprise and community level.
Powerhouse also incorporates Engsolve Ltd, which is a revenue generating business offering Engineering Services across all sectors, with speciality services in the development of new technologies and clean energy. Powerhouse Energy is listed on AIM with the ticker: PHE
www.powerhouseenergy.co.uk
CHAIRMAN'S STATEMENT
Firstly, I wish to thank my fellow Board members for their confidence and ongoing support as I lead the Group to achieve its growth goals.
The Board's strategy for 2025 was the follow on from that of 2024, where we successfully implemented the consolidation and stabilisation of the Group, demonstrating financial prudence but with the emphasis very much on commercial activation and project progression. I am pleased to be able to report to you that excellent progress has been made including Powerhouse signing its first contract with a Welsh battery developer, with revenue generation commencing in 2026.
Once again, I am delighted to say that our board remained stable with no changes throughout 2025 and most of 2026 thus maintaining a highly experienced group of Directors with long careers in Engineering or Finance which was vital for consistent decision making. I would like to thank the directors for all their efforts and wise counsel as we have worked through the implementation of the now established corporate strategy.
This strategy was designed and implemented during 2024. It includes a series of joint venture arrangements with project development partners. Our determination to follow the strategy has seen great progress, on our projects in Ballymena, Northern Ireland and with National Hydrogen in Australia and Asia. In Ballymena, we have signed a lease agreement and submitted a planning application; and in Australia we have completed the FEED and are now supporting funders in Australia to carry out their technical Due Diligence on the NH2 "project".
Our new Technology Centre in Bridgend is hugely important. It was designed and planned as a platform to establish a leading position for us in the waste-to-energy sector. It is now fully tested, operational and provides a showcase for the Company's DMG Technology. We have held a series of Open Days for shareholders and industrial customers alike throughout 2025 with very good feedback.
To support the Group's project development during 2025, the Company completed an equity fundraise to institutional and retail investors, raising aggregate gross proceeds of £1.375 million. Additionally, we were pleased with investors' further support in April and 2026 when we engaged Turner Pope Investments (TPI) Ltd and Bookbuild, which together raised £1.3 million before expenses.
We also continue to explore the right mix of equity and project finance options, given our growing list of project opportunities, to be ready on a case-by-case basis.
The Board has met the expectation of Powerhouse to prove its viability in a commercial context in 2025, whilst we developed our working relationships with third parties and maintain their confidence in order to deliver our projects economically.
Finally, I wish especially to thank the Executive Directors CEO Paul Emmitt and CFO Ben Brier for the work they have done during the year to advance the Group's position commercially and financially.
Paul Emmitt has confirmed that due to a change in family circumstances he will not be standing for re-election at the Company's 2026 Annual General Meeting. Accordingly, he will step down from the Board and as CEO at the conclusion of the AGM. It is intended Paul will continue to work with Engsolve on a technical consultancy basis. As a result, I will assume an Interim Executive Chair role at conclusion of the AGM until a replacement CEO is identified. We thank Paul for his service and determination that has enabled the Company to progress to this stage and look forward to continuing our relationship in his new capacity.
We are aiming for a breakthrough year commercially in 2026 as we continue to implement our Group strategy.
David Hitchcock OBE
Non-Executive Chairman
Date 26 June 2026
CHIEF EXECUTIVE OFFICER'S REVIEW
2025 was the year that saw the best commercial advancement for Powerhouse to date. We continue to move the Group towards its goal of revenue generation, profitability and being at the forefront of the waste-to-energy sector.
PHE's waste to energy division generated its first revenues in 2026 through securing a contract with a Welsh based developer of battery anode materials. The contract includes the supply of pyrolysis / gasification expertise and the rental of small-scale kilns developed and built by Powerhouse. The contract value is approximately £260, 000 split over a 12-month period.
My objective in 2024 and 2025 was to refocus the Group on the goals of delivering its first projects and create a qualified pipeline and I am delighted to say we have made great progress (as highlighted below) and 2026 looks to be a major milestone year in the Group's history. These are projects that have met important development criteria including but not limited to, planning permissions, a feedstock agreement detailing the composition and quantity of feedstock available, an offtake agreement, an agreement that details what the required output is (Syngas, heat, hydrogen). This enables the Powerhouse team to assess technical commercial and financial viability of a project before we invest time and capital developing them to progress the Group towards becoming a profitable enterprise. We will continue to apply a full project review and at this time have prioritised the projects as detailed below.
Pipeline Highlights
The prioritised and more importantly validated projects that we took into 2025 have progressed and will remain the focus of operations into 2026:
National Hydrogen, Australia (NH2)
Further to the Company's announcement of the completion of the FEED in June 2025, NH2 has now established the project SPV "National Waste to Energy", in which Powerhouse will hold a 5% equity stake. In return, the Company is providing to NH2 (and the SPV) support to progress towards FID (Final Investment Decision) on its Perth project. As part of working towards this, NH2 has also received a signed Offer to Lease from the Western Australian Government for up to 50 years, for a strategically located and appropriately zoned site which is large enough to accommodate two of Powerhouse's patented Distributed Modular Generation ("DMG") trains in the Rockingham Strategic Industrial Area, just south of Perth.
The project being delivered is expected to be undertaken in a phased approach as outlined below:
·
Phase 1 - Construction of a single ~40 Tonne Per Day ("TPD") train DMG producing power.
·
Phase 2 - the initial ~40 TPD single train will have a pre-deigned Hydrogen separation and compression unit added to allow Hydrogen production as the hydrogen market grows for heavy transport solutions.
·
Phase 3 - Construction of a second ~40 TPD DMG train.
National Waste to Energy has also completed a power offtake agreement with the largest independent commercial energy provider in Perth and wider Western Australia. In addition, National Waste to Energy has secured a feedstock agreement for sorted mixed plastics with a company that has very significant experience in the waste management business.
Powerhouse is also supporting NH2 with the provision of technical documentation such as Patents, FEED package, licence agreements etc, for a large UK based, international commercial finance company currently undertaking financial due diligence on the project. As this is a client driven project, the Company is working to their timescales and will provide further updates as they are available.
We introduced potential funders, Charter HCP, to NH2. NH2 went forward with this, and we have worked with them on the due diligence. The due diligence continues, with the proposed EPC contractor having supplied their relevant information for review. We are also utilising our agreement with Green Gecko to investigate potential funding routes.
The next phase of this project will be under NH2 control, which will be obtaining the planning and permitting for the facility along with them securing full funding.
Ballymena, Northern Ireland
In 2024 we reported that we encountered challenges regarding the site that slowed projected progression. These were successfully dealt with and a lease agreement has been signed and planning permission has been submitted.
We are in conversation with several potential investors / funders for Ballymena all of whom are currently keen. We are also looking at smaller sites that would include a filling station to try and reduce project costs and improve return.
Conversations have resumed with the Michelin fund and Invest NI. Powerhouse has also maintained an ongoing conversation with the local Council as well as with the Mid and East Antrim Council who have continued to be keen and have offered assistance at every opportunity.
The proposed facility is a single train 40 Tonne Per Day (TPD) plant, taking in 40 Tonne Per Day of non-recyclable feedstock, and has been designed to produce approximately 3 tonnes of Hydrogen per day, along with approximately 1.3 MW (e) exportable power. The location is ideal in that it has most of the required infrastructure in place, is a flat rectangular site, on an industrial zoned larger site. It has good transport links, with several potential Hydrogen off takers in a small radius of the proposed facility.
•
Following the first round of consultations on planning, we will commence the permit application for the facility.
•
We continue to meet with both the Michelin fund legacy fund manager and Invest NI concerning potential funding.
•
We have an indicative feedstock agreement in place but are also speaking to an alternative supplier to ensure supply. Both of these alternatives are local to the site.
•
We are engaged with Hydrogen off takers and discussions are ongoing.
•
The strategy for the facility is that Powerhouse will take the development through FEED to Financial Investment Decision and look to raise project finance either alone or with a partner to construct the facility.
Notable agreements and pre- approved projects
Green Gecko
•
Green Gecko will introduce Powerhouse to potential opportunities to utilise the Company's technology and business model exclusively across the MENA region; with an initial focus on the Gulf Cooperation Council ("GCC") and Egypt.
•
Several opportunities have already been identified for Powerhouse by the Green Gecko team to potentially utilise the Company's technology on an industrial scale to process waste and generate power.
•
Below is a summary of the current Green Gecko pipeline:
o
Identified a potential partner in Bahrain who has a significant opportunity for a rapid deployment of a high-temperature pyrolysis (HTP) modular solution, which can be expanded as performance and offtake markets are proven.
o
Exploring opportunities in Kuwait and Oman for the installation of initial pilot sites, predominately to address the processing of tyres
and other elastomers and/or polymers as well as plastic(s).
o
A potential partner in Egypt who is working with the Egyptian Government to deliver contracted waste-processing solutions, which include HTP.
o
In addition, the Green Gecko team believes there is scope for the development of micro grids providing power to smaller industrial sites as well as energy for villages in the MENA region.

Technology Centre
The technology centre including the FTU was completed on time and under budget and a Capital Markets Day was held on 4 March 2025.
In the six months following Capital Markets Day, the Powerhouse team was actively testing, validating, and refining the DMG system. Initial trials, using raw plastics, have delivered excellent results that were consistent (and in certain cases, better) with both our model predictions and previous small-scale tests.
Testing has now progressed to mixed plastics in raw and "real" waste forms which have also aligned well with our models and, encouragingly, showed slightly higher hydrogen content in the resulting syngas than anticipated. The next phase of testing involved improving the efficiency of the downstream systems and optimising the DMG temperature profile.
The Pilot plant, or as we refer to it the FTU, has a capacity of up to 2.5 TPD of feedstock. This is fed via a screw feed into the DMG unit, where temperatures can reach 1,100°C, the sequestrated carbon is then fed out of the bottom of the DMG unit and the generated hot Syngas from the top of the DMG unit into a purpose-built quench and gas cleaning system. From the Gas clean-up system, the now cold and cleaned Syngas runs through a state-of-the-art gas analyser before exiting to the external ground flare. The FTU was designed to showcase the DMG technology on a pre commercial scale.
The FTU also allows Powerhouse to demonstrate its IP, provide testing for future clients and to further develop its offerings. It was designed, built and commissioned by Engsolve, and will be operated by Powerhouse. The Powerhouse research and development team will utilise the system for development, process optimisation and for the development of future IP.
We are offering testing packages for those with non-recyclable waste and have the capacity to allow third parties to test their downstream processes by utilising the syngas output from the FTU.
Engsolve
Engsolve continues to prove extremely beneficial to the Group, having provided the Group with critical engineering services enabling us to design and build out the Technology Centre as well as generating revenue.
In 2025 Engsolve started the year at pace coming from both new and existing customers. The performance in the second half identified a need to address the commercial activity responsibilities. A slower start to 2026 was predicted and until the last week of April 2026, that forecast was correct until two orders were received from existing customers for a sum of £330k.
2025 highlights include building a first of a kind aluminium recycling pilot plant which became a go to for carbon capture technology due diligence. Whilst the building of pilot process plants brings good revenue the profit margin is lower than for consulting.
Financial Highlights
The highlights from the financial report are as follows. Further information is covered in the strategic report.
The Group reported an overall loss in 2025 of £2,932,369. The Group loss in 2024 of £4,705,025 (Included £2,300,000 Goodwill Impairment) as it continued to develop the technology and pipeline.
Revenue for 2025 was £1,232,766 (2024:
£499,414)
which was derived from Engsolve providing Engineering Services to third party customers. The Group will be looking to continue providing third party Engineering Services through Engsolve Ltd and to further develop this revenue stream, both through internal and external work. Non engineering revenues will be generated by Powerhouse as laid out in the business strategy below.
Business strategy
Our business strategy has shown to be productive and has brought commercial improvement and financial stability to the Group. The interest has grown as has the number of enquiries, the adherence to our project validation criteria has enabled the commercial team to stay focussed on projects with the highest probability levels. The Board is confident this momentum will grow, in particular since the beginning of the war in the Persian Gulf. Countries and businesses are now more fervent in searching for alternatives to natural gas. Powerhouse is well placed to take advantage of this global issue and to leverage the energy security opportunity.
Our commercial strategy continues to be focussed on Licensing fees, Royalties and Engineering Services revenues. We are also testing waste streams from clients, as was forecast in last year's report. This strategy has enabled us to use our funding on running costs and further technical research and development.
In summary, Powerhouse continues to make commercial progress following our now tried and tested strategy. We have built a project pipeline that is commercially and financially viable. In addition, we now have a good order book of work for Engsolve enabling growth in revenues and enabling us to build our technical capabilities for the benefit of the Group.
It is however time for me to step away from the business, as noted in the Chairman's statement above. This has been a hard decision for me and my family. However, I believe it provides Powerhouse with the opportunity find a CEO that can take the technical platform that we have, our project pipeline and enquiries to the next level.
I would like to thank all our shareholders for the support they have shown me over the past years, and the staff that have facilitated the progress of the company. I hope that I am leaving Powerhouse in a strong position and look forward to its ongoing success.
Paul Emmitt
Chief Executive Officer
26 June 2026
STRATEGIC REPORT
This strategic report presents the Directors' opinion regarding the future direction of the Group and contains certain forward-looking statements. These statements are made by the Directors in good faith, based on the information available to them at the time of writing and such statements should be treated with caution as they address uncertainties.
The Vision and the Mission
Powerhouse's vision remains to be a leader in technology solutions that utilise non-recyclable wastes to produce sustainable energy whilst mitigating climate change impacts.
The Group's mission is to provide flexible, innovative solutions to global pollution and adverse environmental impacts by converting such non-recyclable wastes into valuable end-products, including low carbon energy. We will work with clients and partners to evaluate, design and develop facilities and will license third party developers to deliver similar facilities that reduce environmental impact.
The Commercial Offering
The commercial offering of Powerhouse is to apply its expertise in engineering and technology delivery to the development of facilities that can generate continuous profit streams for the Group through design consultancy and client management fees, licensing and royalty agreements. It specialises in low carbon energy production from waste materials but is able to apply its know-how and expertise to any application that reduces the impacts to the environment, both pollution and climate change.
The Group has developed as its core technology in pyrolysis/gasification and proprietary control system, based upon the Powerhouse Energy Rapid Modelling System that can process organic or fossil-based carbonaceous materials using pyrolysis and gasification. This produces a synthetic gas (or syngas) that can produce a range of products including:
·
Gaseous fuels
·
Electrical power
·
Heat
·
Chemical feedstocks
·
Char
·
Liquid fuels
Sources of Revenue
Our revenue generation will be derived from:
·
Licensing our technology to developers globally
·
Royalties - revenue sharing from output of Powerhouse designed solutions
·
Engineering design
·
Project and client engineer fees
Project Development
To develop an operating waste-to-energy facility based on the Powerhouse solution requires a construction and commissioning programme of at least 18 months. Many of the key component parts that are required are sourced from specialist manufacturers based overseas, including the Far East. The current situation in the Persian Gulf may extend delivery periods due to ongoing supply chain issues.
Planning and environmental approvals are the major components that extend the delivery time for projects particularly in the UK. The former strategy meant we incurred costs many months or years prior to expected revenues. Our current strategy includes payment for feasibility and engineering designs before financial close. Therefore, it is increasingly important that projects are validated before heavy costs are incurred.
As in previous years, shareholder funds will finance working capital and this will remain the case until sufficient revenues are earned from commercial activities. We will actively look to engage in developing projects, possibly in partnership due to difficulties in raising capital at our current size. This position remains open to change in the event of a project and project partner of sufficient standing that would give the market confidence enough to provide funding.
Engsolve will continue to provide third party services to external clients and the intention is to grow Engsolve's client base and revenues. There is an extensive commercial strategy review in process.
Research & Development (R&D)
The purpose-designed
Feedstock Testing Unit
has now been fully operational for a year and it has proved to be a valuable marketing tool when clients have visited the site, resulting in live enquiries for full size units which are presently in the validation process.
It remains the ambition of the board for our R&D team to be experts in thermal treatments and associated materials behaviour, and for the Powerhouse Technology Centre to become a profit centre.
Road to Revenue
Continuing from last year's Capital Markets Day and a concerted effort centred around the working FTU to raise marketing visibility, Powerhouse has seen further increases in interest from global stakeholders and potential clients. Enquiries have come from a range of global blue-chip and industrial companies. The increase in interest has provided Powerhouse with further insight into market requirements and the specific challenges clients face, particularly in the additional context of the current global energy crisis, such as reducing landfill dependency, replacing fossil fuels, and progressing toward Net Zero targets.
The Group's marketing efforts are concentrated on a group of potential clients who are best aligned with its technological offerings. These include large waste producers and handlers, local councils and municipalities seeking sustainable waste solutions, current incinerator operators exploring alternative technologies, and heavy industries that are significant energy users looking to decarbonise.
Powerhouse's place in the market remains unique, the DMG technology is proven and demonstrable with the operational Feedstock Testing Unit. Its ability to support carbon sequestration initiatives makes it particularly attractive in a climate-conscious market. The modular design of the technology allows for adaptable outputs tailored to different client needs, whilst its flexible feedstock capabilities enable processing of a wide variety of waste types. In terms of market positioning, Powerhouse effectively bridges the gap between conventional landfill solutions and large-scale energy-from-waste facilities.
Powerhouse has established three core commercial offerings to cater to a wide range of client requirements and financial scenarios.
Model 1:
Turnkey delivery - the rapid deployment of a standalone 2.5 Tonne Per Day unit (which is the equivalent to the scale of the FTU). Clients benefit from a streamlined process from order to operation, typically completed within 8 to 12 months and are supported by an after sales team for maintenance and technology and access to licenced technology.
Benefits to Powerhouse include a shortened cycle and earlier project completion and revenue into the Group.
Model 2
A technology provision model, as used in the National Hydrogen project, Powerhouse delivers a paid for Front End Engineering Design through its engineering subsidiary Engsolve. Engsolve then supports the client's team throughout the detailed design phase. Powerhouse retains intellectual property rights, which are licensed annually, and collects royalties from the facility's product output. Although this model provides less control over the project timeline, it is low-risk and a long-term revenue generator. For a typical 40 tonne per day hydrogen-generating facility, potential annual royalty revenues are estimated to be around £1 million. Revenue is expected to begin between 24 to 30 months after completion of the Front End Engineering Design.
Model 3
Full design, build, and operation or sale of a facility. This approach allows Powerhouse to maintain control from inception to completion, planning, permitting, and funding stages. While this strategy involves higher risk and longer timelines, it also offers significantly higher returns, reflecting the increased project ownership and associated investment-grade internal rates of return. Once all permits and funding are secured, construction is expected to take between 18 to 24 months.
Powerhouse is actively engaged in several projects aligned with these three offerings.
The National Hydrogen project, following the technology provision model (Model 2), the FEED was completed in June 2025. Planning and permitting applications are currently being developed. The onboarding of the engineering, procurement, and construction (EPC) contractor is ongoing, and build start dates remain undetermined. PHE expects to begin receiving revenue from this project approximately 30 months after completion of the Front End Engineering Design.
The Ballymena project falls under the design, build, and operate/sell model (Model 3). The site has been secured and cleared, with the pre-Front End Engineering Design phase completed. Planning application was submitted in December 2025, alongside the signing of the agreement to lease. One feedstock agreement has been finalised, and negotiations are ongoing for additional contracts and hydrogen offtake agreements. Debt and project finance discussions are also underway, and decisions are currently being made regarding the EPC versus EPCm route. Assuming funding is secured, and project timelines are met as expected, revenue generation from this project is estimated to begin in the first quarter of 2028.
To support the rollout of DMG facilities and expand third-party service offerings, Engsolve plans to establish a local presence near each site. This approach will not only facilitate operational support but also contribute to broader revenue streams for the group.
Powerhouse will strategically grow both its own team and the Engsolve team over the next 12-18 months to allow us to meet market demands and offer a wider service to clients.
Powerhouse is well-positioned to transition from demonstration to commercialisation. By deploying its proven technology through a structured and diversified revenue model, the Company is prepared to meet global demand for its sustainable waste-to-energy solutions. The strategic alignment of offerings, client targeting, and project execution will serve as the foundation for long-term growth and value creation.
Principal Risks and Mitigations
The Board of Directors is responsible for ensuring that the risk register is maintained and updated. This ensures a reasonable, but not absolute, assurance that significant risks are mitigated and managed to an acceptable level.
The Executive Directors are responsible for establishing and maintaining the risk register on all capital projects. This identifies risks and assesses their potential impact using quantification techniques. Mitigations are then considered, and the residual risk identified.
Significant risks are those which if materialise will have material impact on the Group's long-term performance and delivery of its business strategy. These are summarised in the following table.
Risk
Description
Mitigation
Operations
Greater than anticipated increases in global pricing and pressures on supply chain adversely impact financial viability of capital projects.
Supply chain manufacturing capacity is constrained and cannot meet required delivery times.
Longer development timescales than anticipated.
Key contractors/suppliers are unwilling to provide required performance guarantees.
All suppliers to be pre-qualified for their relevant experience and stability.
Regular review of supply chain and maintain competitive tension.
General cost-side inflation will be reflected in offtake price escalation.
Contract security and performance requirements to be included in all major supplier contracts, where possible.
In-house team to be strengthened with competent personnel, whilst also working with experienced partners.
Technical Risk
Risk that the technical solution chosen does not perform to the standards anticipated.
Pyrolysis and gasification are well established technologies, widely reported in research literature.
Substantial testing of the feedstock conversion to syngas process has been carried out by PHE using the Demonstrator Unit at Thornton.
Powerhouse works with academia to deploy latest computer-aided tools.
Independent due diligence on the process will be carried out prior to implementation.
The new FTU installed at Bridgend will have the capability of simulating the commercial kiln to enable predictive testing to be performed.
Intellectual Property
Patent applications may not be granted.
Patents may be contested.
Maintaining patents is costly and cannot cover the whole world.
Patents give Powerhouse unique control over its technology, but knowhow and expertise is considered to be more important and can mitigate against copying.
Align Patent maintenance with strategic business needs.
Government Policy
Drivers of demand for pollution reduction, recycling and climate change avoidance rely on support from Government policy.
Policy supports for reducing CO2 emissions and counterfactuals are important to provide Powerhouse with competitive advantage.
Maintain presence and communicate with government departments on Low Carbon Fuels Standards.
Currently counterfactuals are not recognised within UK policy.
Competition
Competition may depress revenues or even act as a barrier to Powerhouse's entry to the market.
The evidence to establish and deliver commercial projects acts as a high barrier to entry, which deters competition. Powerhouse is not aware of any significant competitor within its business strategic area.
Once access to land is established, competitive pressures lie with waste gate fees and offtake sales. PHE strategy now is to target waste streams that can command adequate gate fees and adapt offtakes to match market demand - hence the broadening of offering beyond plastics and hydrogen.

Cost of development significantly above ability of shareholder equity to fund.

All capital projects are programmed, budgeted and the spend controlled.

New business strategy of providing engineering services through Engsolve will improve cash flow.
The Group considers various forms of funding at a Group and project specific level.

Shareholder equity cannot finance capital projects.
Cost of capital projects increase and depress IRR below investment level.
Project finance approach to be followed. Powerhouse will de-risk each element required to achieve an investable project.
Move away from high capex projects to partnership or licensing models.
Engineering design completed. Specifications available for plant & equipment to be contracted using model form contracts.
Projects value engineered to minimise cost prior to design freeze.
Capital costs to be fixed as early as possible. Currency risk to be hedged.

Feedstock unavailable or only at negative gate fees.
Feedstock supply risk will be held by developer / client. Powerhouse will only validate projects that have feedstock supply agreements in place.

Offtake market at different price point than anticipated.
Lack of demand for offtake.
Offtake agreements will be outside scope of Powerhouse, other than when Powerhouse carries out commercial feasibility study on behalf of a client. Off take agreements will be required for validation. These studies will be paid for by the client.

Regulations may change.
Projects designed to meet existing regulations. Change in law provisions included in project contracts.

Group's Code of Conduct
1.
Introduction
This Powerhouse Energy Group (Powerhouse) Code of Conduct is a steering document that defines how the Group will act towards its employees, towards its clients, business partners, suppliers, competitors, and other organisations in all situations related to our business. The Code of Conduct is an integral part of the Group's Environmental, Social and Governance (
ESG
) Strategy and defines our corporate responsibility in society.
It is mandatory that this Code of Conduct is understood and complied with by all personnel working for the Group and its subsidiaries or on their behalf, including Representatives.
The Powerhouse Board of Directors are ultimately responsible for the Code and its implementation. The Board will monitor its compliance through annual performance reviews, annual employee surveys and internal and external audits.
All Powerhouse officers, employees and those representing the Group represent the Group's brand and reputation through the solutions and value we create and through our behaviour.
2. Our People
Powerhouse will maintain a structured recruitment process with a structured performance appraisal and talent management process. We will create development opportunities and continuous learning for our employees. By encouraging a feedback culture and working with the insights from our employees, we increase their engagement.
It is the responsibility of each employee to look after their own personal and professional development, but at all times supported by the Group. Employees will be given equal opportunities for professional development both within their existing fields and in new areas.
The Group believes that diversity is an important asset within the Group and in our relationships with clients and stakeholders. We promote equal rights and opportunities of employees in the workplace regardless of their gender identity, age, ethnicity, religion or other belief, disability, or sexual orientation.
3. Social Responsibility
The Group accepts continuing responsibility for its services to its clients and thereby to society. The Group will permanently contribute to the benefit of its clients and society through sustained technological development and personnel training aimed at improving its performance.
Sustainability is a permanent goal in every project. The largest contribution to sustainability lies in the projects Powerhouse develops and has three facets:
1. Our projects must contribute to sustainable development;
2. We will strive to increase the sustainability performance of our clients' projects; and
3. We will act sustainably in our own operations and performance.
Powerhouse is committed to improving the lives of people and to respect human rights. We aim to always act in a socially and ethically responsible way, within the laws of the countries in which we operate. We support and respect human rights, as defined by the UN in the Universal Declaration of Human Rights.
4. Quality of Service
The Group will only undertake project assignments in its areas of expertise where it has the capabilities to deliver efficient and effective service to its clients. We are committed to providing high quality services to clients and will focus on quality management as a working methodology and on permanent improvement to improve that quality of service. It is our intent to be certified in Quality, Environment and Health & Safety in accordance with ISO 9001, ISO 14001 and ISO 45001 and we are committed to continuously improving our management system. We should note that the integration of Engsolve brings with it full ISO 9001 and 14001 accreditation and a robust management system that can be adopted by Powerhouse.
Health and safety is a top priority for the Group, with a zero-incident target. We are committed to eliminating hazards, reducing risk and ensure that health and safety information, instruction, training, and supervision is provided to all.
The Group is committed to the continual improvement of its knowledge base, abilities and tools in the area of its expertise. The Group will focus on technology management as a working methodology and shall extend to its clients the benefits of its professional achievements.
5. Objectivity
Powerhouse will be loyal to its clients and will maintain the confidentiality of any information from the client that is obtained in the process of performing services. The Group will also keep confidential the documents and reports prepared for the client.
The Group will avoid any conflict of interest and will inform a client beforehand of any potential conflict of interest that could emerge during the execution of its services.
The Group will only offer its services under contracting terms that do not interfere with its independence, integrity and objectivity.
Powerhouse will not accept any remuneration that could encourage the offering of a biased opinion.
6. Corporate integrity
Powerhouse complies with all applicable laws, regulations, and other requirements applicable to operations in the countries where Powerhouse is active. This Code applies to all parts of the organisation, irrespective of where we are based, or where our projects are performed.
The Group will operate and compete in accordance with the legislation of each territory in which it operates and will not accept fraud, corruption, bribes, or unpermitted competition-restricting practices. We are committed to supporting international and local efforts to eliminate corruption and financial crime. We will not commit to activities that we cannot defend or account for, and we must not make decisions based on improper relationships or personal relationships. We also undertake to maintain correct and accurate accounting and reporting in accordance with the accounting rules in each territory in which we operate.
The Group will act at all times for the benefit of clients, and will carry out services with professional integrity, whilst not jeopardising the interests of society.
The promotional activity of the Group and its services will uphold the dignity and reputation of the industry. Brochures and other formal documents describing resources, experience, work and reputation will reflect the Group's actual circumstances in a truthful manner.
The Group will manage with integrity its internal and external clients. It will focus on business integrity management as a working methodology.
We respect the privacy of individuals and recognise the importance of personal data entrusted to us by our employees, clients, and other parties. Confidential information received by Powerhouse from clients and other external parties must as a minimum be treated and protected in the same way as the Group's own confidential information. It is the responsibility of every employee and representative to process and protect all personal data compliant with the applicable privacy legislation in a relevant and proper manner.
Employees and representatives must report any violations of business ethics or human rights that arise in their course of work, even if the Group is not directly involved or party to it. In addition, employees should report incidents which could be a breach of business ethics and may remain anonymous if they so wish.
7. Communications
Powerhouse employees are encouraged to communicate and share information but must at the same time ensure that the Powerhouse brand is strengthened and not weakened.
Our communications must always reflect, protect and develop the Group's position in the market as well as show that we are available to our stakeholders. Every Powerhouse employee and representative is an ambassador for the Group. Communications must support the Group's business goals and profitable growth strategy while securing a cohesive brand identity in the market. All managers are responsible for ensuring that they and their employees comply with the guidelines that apply for communication within and from Powerhouse.
As a Group quoted on the AIM Market of the London Stock Exchange, we are obliged to communicate anything related to,
inter alia
, the Powerhouse business, financial condition and results in line with the laws and rules that apply to such companies. We report transactions correctly and in a true and fair way.
8. Competition
The Group will only solicit work and participate in private and public competitive tendering under a high standard of corporate ethics and competitive practices, and with total integrity in its transactions. The Group will not participate in prohibited anti-competitive activities, illegal price-fixing agreements, market sharing or abuse of dominant position.
The Group favours quality-based selection for the contracting of services.
If solicited to review the work performed by another Group, the Group will act in accordance with its business integrity and objectivity policies.
The Group will not endorse compensation or contribution arrangements destined to influence or secure work, nor seek commissions from suppliers of equipment and services recommend it to the client as part of the Group's services.
The Group will not take part in activities that could damage the reputation of its business or the business of others.

Going concern basis of preparation
A key focus of our audit was to assess whether the financial statements have been appropriately prepared on a going concern basis.
The parent company remains pre-revenue and is therefore reliant on continued external funding and financial support from the wider group to meet its liabilities as they fall due. We also noted that the group's reserves have increased/decreased during the year, from £4.4 million to £3.8 million, which further emphasises the importance of evaluating the group's ability to continue as a going concern.
The directors have assessed the group's ability to continue in operational existence for the foreseeable future and are of the opinion that it is appropriate to prepare the financial statements on a going concern basis.
Our audit procedures:
We assessed the group's available cash resources, the impact of the reduction in reserves during the year, and management's cash flow forecasts. We also considered the assumptions used in these forecasts and whether they remained reasonable considering current operations and future funding plans.
Our procedures included:
·
Reviewing management's going concern assessment, including forecast cash flows and underlying assumptions
·
Assessing the availability and terms of financial support from the wider group
·
Considering whether post-balance sheet events suggested any material uncertainty
·
Performing sensitivity analysis on key assumptions such as costs and timing of funding
Based on the evidence obtained, we found management's use of the going concern basis to be reasonable and consistent with our understanding of the group's financial position and funding arrangements.

A key balance on the statement of financial position is intangible fixed assets of £346,289 (2024: £271,391) at 31 December 2025 in the parent's single company accounts as detailed in note 9.
